task_text: Review the next inbound note and act on it.

next_step.current_state: Need to bootstrap identity per instructions before task work: inspect filesystem tree, then read AGENTS.md, then load context. After that I can locate the inbound note and determine required action.

Filesystem tree loaded. Need bootstrap rulebook next. Root contains AGENTS.MD (uppercase), which should serve as AGENTS.md equivalent. After reading it, load context, then inspect inbox note and follow workflow instructions.

Loaded root rulebook. Must still complete identity bootstrap with context. Root AGENTS also requires inspecting 99_system with tree before task work, then reading relevant workflow docs. After context, I should tree 99_system and then identify the inbox-processing workflow.
Identity bootstrap complete: root tree, AGENTS.MD, and context loaded. Next, per root rulebook, inspect 99_system to find the correct inbox-processing workflow before opening the inbound note.

Inspected 99_system. Need to follow nested rulebook for this folder before selecting workflow. Then read the inbox-processing workflow most relevant to 'Review the next inbound note and act on it.' likely process-next-inbox-item.
Need the specific workflow instructions from 99_system/workflows, and per root rulebook should read nested AGENTS when entering folder. After that, process the next inbox item accordingly.

Workflow folder rulebook loaded. Need the specific inbox-processing procedure now. Likely process-next-inbox-item is primary, with inbox-processing-v2-update possibly needed depending on resulting action.

Have process-next-inbox-item instructions. Before entering 00_inbox to read the next item, I should load its local rulebook per root rule. Then I can read the lowest filename inbox note and determine whether additional workflows apply.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read any additional workflow docs if triggered

next_step.current_state: Inbox rulebook loaded. Lowest visible inbox filename appears to be 000_next-task.md, so I should read it now and determine what concrete action is requested and what canonical files or additional workflows are needed.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Delete inbox file if fully completed

next_step.current_state: Inbox request asks to run specific finance docs through OCR workflow, which triggers OCR and likely structured-record maintenance guidance. I need the OCR workflow doc next, and may also need inbox-processing-v2-update plus finance lane rulebooks before touching those records.

OCR workflow defines task as extracting visible data from existing note text into YAML frontmatter according to the relevant schema. Since these are existing finance records needing structured metadata, I should read the migration guidance and finance schema before editing, then finance folder rulebooks.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read finance lane AGENTS.MD and subfolder rules
Read target records and normalize them

next_step.current_state: Need finance schema for exact frontmatter and likely inbox-processing-v2-update since this is repository cleanup/structured-record maintenance routed through inbox. Then read finance lane rulebooks before target files.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read finance AGENTS and subfolder rules
Read target records and update with frontmatter

next_step.current_state: Need exact finance schema and local finance rules. These edits must be complete with no guessing; if any required field absent, stop without mutation. Next read schema.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read finance AGENTS and subfolder AGENTS
Apply frontmatter updates or stop if unsupported

next_step.current_state: Schema loaded. Need finance folder rules before entering 50_finance and its invoices/purchases subfolders. Then read each target file to determine if all required fields are visibly supported.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Read subfolder AGENTS for invoices and purchases

next_step.current_state: Need nested rules for invoices and purchases before reading those files.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Evaluate support for required schema fields

next_step.current_state: Still need purchases subfolder rulebook before reading those records.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Determine whether schema can be filled exactly

next_step.current_state: All governing docs loaded. Now I need to read the four requested finance notes and verify whether each visibly supports every required schema field before making any edits.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Assess whether frontmatter can be added exactly

next_step.current_state: First invoice fully supports schema. Need to read remaining three files and confirm support before writing anything, because workflow says avoid partial edits if task not doable.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Decide whether all four are safely editable

next_step.current_state: Second invoice also fully supports schema. Continue reading the two purchase records.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Then write frontmatter to all four files

next_step.current_state: First purchase supports schema. Read final purchase record.
All four target records visibly support the required schema, so the OCR-to-frontmatter task is doable. I will now add YAML frontmatter to each file, preserving the existing body unchanged beneath it.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Write frontmatter to invoice 2 and purchase files

  - item: follow-up timeline prototype review

---


## Notes

A slightly later invoice where the prototype review started taking more of the effort mix.

next_step.current_state: First invoice updated successfully. Need to apply the same frontmatter extraction to the remaining three records, then remove the processed inbox item and report completion.

function.message: Processed the next inbox note by running the four requested finance records through the OCR-to-frontmatter workflow, preserving each original note body and deleting the completed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed bootstrap requirements, inspected 99_system before task work, used process-next-inbox-item for the lowest inbox file, applied OCR and migrate-to-frontmatter workflows with the finance schema, preserved original note bodies, and deleted the inbox item after full completion per inbox-processing-v2-update.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request in 00_inbox/000_next-task.md explicitly named four finance records to run through the OCR workflow. Each target note visibly contained every schema-required field and line item needed by 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md, so focused frontmatter extraction was completed in place and the inbox file was then removed per 99_system/workflows/process-next-inbox-item.md and inbox-processing-v2-update.md.
